title: Standardized status column using managed columns but can't filter
excerpt: Hi I created a managed column for status to make sure every project has the same status choices. However, when I created a new board so that I can see all items that are not “done” I can’t use the managed column to filte…

posts.author: MelE (Mel)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi I created a managed column for status to make sure every project has the same status choices. However, when I created a new board so that I can see all items that are not “done” I can’t use the managed column to filter, only the original “status” column. Did I mess up?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
You’ll need to pull in that column template and not just the normal status column. To do so, choose ‘more columns’ at the bottom , then you’ll see ‘column templates’ on the left.

image311×573 18.3 KB

image414×505 24.5 KB

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @MelE
You didn’t mess up

Managed Columns standardize the options, but you can only filter by the column that actually exists on the board you’re viewing. So if your new board still has the original Status column (or the managed one wasn’t added), the filter will only show what’s on that board.

Dr. Tanvi Sachar
Monday Certified Partner, Tuesday Wizard
